comment: Per-card art metadata, keyed by card name. Hand-maintained; merged into art-tracker.html at generation. Fields per entry: 'artist' (who is working on it), 'brief' (art direction notes). Status is DERIVED: artFinal=true in cards.json → Done; artist set here → In Progress; else Placeholder. Example: "Broom Strike": { "artist": "Sam", "brief": "low angle, hat silhouette" }

Defend.brief: This is our base defend card. There will be 5 in every starting deck. - it should depict the witch in a relatively neutral state without upgrades, simply blocking. See other blocks for inspiration - they typically depict the character in portrait holding some sort of equipment while rebuffing streaks of light.

Extract Essence.brief: Depiction of either the character extracting essence from a common enemy or a common enemy simply bring drained of their essence. A relatively evil card. Part of the STANDARD card set, so it will be in your deck at the start of every game.

Strike.brief: This is our base base strike card. There will be 5 in every starting deck. Strikes. Strikes typically depict the 'basic' weapon of the character. For the witch, this will likely be vicious, extended claws.

Bag of Teeth.brief: A bag of teeth! This will be a common payoff to 'big strength' and 'big vigor 'strategies. Should depict either teeth being thrown at an enemy (a little more difficult to read the 'teeth' in) or a bag overflowing with teeth.

Bewitching Grin.brief: A sinister, evil grin with sparkling eyes. Feel free to be evil! This will be an 'intimidation'-themed defensive tool.

Big Batch.brief: 'Potions' are a strong theme in the card set. This common card will serve as a consistent generator. It should depict either a bottling process of two Noxious Brews, or the action process of creating a large quantity of potions.

Bottle Barrage.brief: Bottle Barrage is a massive payoff card for potion strategies. Depict potions hurtling through the air, flung by the witch at her enemies.

Broom Strike.brief: A broom brought down from on high, sweeping enemies away, can depict either scampering mice, or one of the smaller common slay the spire enemies being dispersed. The card itself is not a synergy piece for a large theme, and more a glue card for other decks.

Call the Pack.brief: Familiars ended up having a 'Wolf' sub-archetype, decks focused on generating as many 'Gnash' cards as possible. See Gnash for thematic details. This is a common include for the 'Wolf' deck. It should depict the witch calling a pack of wolves.

Gnash.brief: Gnash is a main payoff and damage dealer for the 'Wolf' sub-archetype. This card is generated by the Wolf familiar, and should depict the Wolf familiar vicious gnashing with bloody teeth and murderous intent

Fertilize.brief: This is a nice glue piece for the Brambles archetype. It should depict nurtured, actively growing, and thorny plants. The plants themselves can either be sinister or tame, but should depict the thorny themes of brambles.

Forbidden Magic.brief: The witch casts a devious spell, sacrificing some of their own power to wallop the enemy with magic. The spell should feel devastating, but the witch should be distressed by casting it.

Herbal Brew.brief: One of the Common Brew trio. This will create a common, valuable utility potion for the player. These potions can draw cards, reduce mana cost, generally any action not directly related to offense or defense. It should feel herbal, mysterious, fluid. Depicts full focus on a single potion bottle.

Hide in a Bush.brief: Depicts the witch hiding in a bush. The thematics on this one can be a bit silly if desired. This is a main defensive card for the 'Brambles' archetype, so please make sure the the bush is thorny!

Lavender and Sage.brief: Depicts garlands of lavender and sage, bundles of the two, thorns strung into the mix. This card should be a bit more peaceful, therapeutic, calming, but prickly!

Needle Whip.brief: The placeholder depiction is quite strong here - an evil, lashing whip of thorns, berries still attached to the brambles. This can either depict the weapon itself or the action of hitting an enemy, though weapon focus gives a much more clear read on the thorns and berries at a distance.

Pact of Agony.brief: The witch stabs/slices a portion of their body, blood rushing out in a nova, cutting enemies in turn. String themes of 'paying the cost' for heavy damage.

Read the Bones.brief: Carved bones lay on the tray, inlays glowing a soft purple as they speak sinister intent. Potential: eyes glow faintly in the background, or a claw sneaks towards the bones to inspect them.

Stony Brew.brief: One of the Common Brew trio. This will create a common defensive potion for the player. These potions create block, give dexterity, and general create 'safety. The bottle should feel sturdy, steadfest, and the liquid could even feel a bit thick. if you drink this potion, you'll definitely be healthy.

Tinder.brief: A Small bundle of sticks and twigs ignite - the witch has spent a bit of their own growth to feed the flames.

Vexing Strike.brief: A slashing claw, fingertips trailing purple. Hexes are sinister, so feel free to play with carved spells or blood.

Weathered Witch Hat.brief: Depiction of a big, worn, patchy witch hat. It should feel comfortable, old, reverent with years of experience. Can be depict either in a peaceful sunlit room, or actively worn by the witch herself.

Wicked Brew.brief: One of the Common Brew trio. This will create a common offensive potion for the player. These potions create attacks, grant strength, and damage enemies. The bottle should feel volatile, aggressive, and even a bit evil. if you use this potion, someone will get hurt.

Bear Familiar.brief:
Familiar. Associated Cards: Hibernate, Mutilate. 
A big ole bear! It should feel sturdy, difficult to move, big trying to do so would probably be a bad idea. Thematically, players will pick this bear when they want a generally solid set of cards which don't synergize with other strategies. Big, beefy value cards.

Bind in Blood.brief: Another 'pay the price' card - depiction of a cost being paid (contract, sacrifice, signature), and purple wispy tendrils curling out of the payment (imminent hexing!)

Brambleburst.brief: A knot of brambles explodes into a torrent of thorns, berries, and vines. It's vicious, self-destructive, and will ultimately destroy the knot of thorns.

Cat Familiar.brief:
Familiar. Associated Cards: Nimble, Ferocity
A lithe, nimble cat. Lanky, quick. It attacks fast, dodges around, and is generally just a naughty little fellow.

Cloak of Moonlight.brief: The witch is shrouded in moonlight, defended. This card is powerful,safe, used in many decks. The moon should feel like your guardian in these trying times.

Creeping Vines.brief: Thorns and vibes seep into a creature, pulling them into the thicket. This could be a creature, the witch herself, or a familiar.

Crystal Bottle.brief: This should depict an empty crystal bottle. The bottle should feel rare, strange, pristine, ready to be filled with a liquid, ready to enhance it! Gameplay-wise, this will serve as a 'build your own cool buff'

Deep Roots.brief: General themes of growth, thickets, brambles, or roots piercing into the earth. This is a generator card which consistently enables the brambles synergy.

Distill.brief: Depictions of a brewing apparatus hooked into potions, seeking to distill powerful potions into *even more powerful versions*. Should feel experimental, controlled, and intentional, like a fantasy chemical distillation.

Eye of Newt.brief: Should depict the witch adding additional ingredients to their potions, making them more powerful, more evil! Doesn't necessarily need to be an eye of newt added to a potion.

Feast With Wolves.brief: The 'Wolf' archetype should feel feral, the witch joining into the gnashing and biting the wolves represent. This is a more 'general' card, but it fits the wolf thematic quite well.

Grind Down.brief: A Jeweled mortal and pestle grinds ingredients down! The placeholder is pretty spot on here - have fun with it!.

Light the Candle.brief: A close up of one or more candles being lit against a dark background. If anything is in the background, it should be subtle and mysterious.

Hasty Brew.brief: The witch creates a fast brew! This could simply depict the energy potion, or a potion that simply *feels* fast. Either way, it should depict a yellow liquid to adhere with the Slay the Spire color language.

Overrun.brief: A horde of familiars charge at the enemy! Witch familiars include Bear, Crow, Rat, Wolf, Owl and Cat.

Owl Familiar.brief:
Familiar. Associated Cards: Knowledge, Wisdom
A prideful, knowledgeable owl. He can be old and wizened, or young and and prideful. Either way, the owl is learned, creates cards that spread knowledge, and is a generally helpful companion in accelerating card draw.

Pact of Fury.brief: Should depict the witch sacrificing something to empower the Ironclad and Necrobinder. Multiplayer-only card - will be played in a team setting.

Pick Clean.brief: A group of familiars bite at a common STS2 enemy. It doesn't need to be gruesome, but the enemy should at least look a bit sad about the whole ordeal.

Prices Paid.brief: More paying of the price! But this time, the witch is paying the price to create a noxious brew. This one I'm personally struggling to come up with visuals for mentally, so go nuts :)!

Refuse Pile.brief: The witch hides behind a giant pile of refuse, friendly rats crawling all over it. These rats will hop into the witch's deck.

Ritual Sacrifice.brief: A sad, tragic card. The witch sacrifices a familiar for her own power. It's bound and pensive, sitting in a circle, having accepted its fate.

Rotting Roots.brief: This one's a more general card - enemies rot and wither away, their fates turning sour. Frankly, the thematic of the card effect vs the current name and visuals are a bit suspect, but this one should depict soemthing withering away slowly.
